criteria_map: Identify current call-time assembly path -> covered under "Current Implementation" in R000.
Explain current cost/latency source -> covered by on-demand DFS rebuild and mutation/read path analysis.
Provide multiple design options -> six options listed.
Compare correctness/latency/complexity/recovery/fit -> each option includes pros, cons, and best use.
Recommend direction -> phased recommendation selects folded-prefix projection plus active-path on-demand, with DFS fallback.
evidence: Source pointers inspected: Runtime prepare handler, context read/append handlers, CortexBridge prepare path, Cortex API prepare/append/step/scope endpoints, Workspace mutation methods, ContextEngine and StepTreeBuilder.
Result R000 documents current call-time assembly, mutation points, six options, pros/cons, and recommended phased direction.
execution_map: T000 -> completed via source inspection and design analysis, recorded as R000.
residual_risk: This is design research only; implementation tickets would still need detailed schemas for generation metadata, projection files, repair paths, and tests.
stress_test: Stale projection failure mode -> recommendation requires generation checks and DFS fallback.
Notification boundary failure mode -> recommendation preserves notification-as-hint semantics; no raw IM bodies are introduced into projections.
Projection worker lag failure mode -> async option requires wait/fallback/repair handling rather than silent stale reads.
Over-eager write-through failure mode -> synchronous write-through is explicitly not recommended as primary path.
C000.summary: The research problem is satisfied: current implementation was inspected from source, mutation points were mapped, six design options were compared, and a phased recommendation was recorded.

P000.description: Research the current novaic-cortex LLM context assembly implementation and evaluate whether context can be assembled before the LLM call or maintained incrementally instead of rebuilding from DFS state only during `prepare_for_llm`.
success_criteria: Identify the current call-time assembly path and the write events that mutate context state.
Explain why the current implementation assembles on demand and where the cost/latency comes from.
Provide multiple design options for non-call-time or incremental assembly.
Compare each option by correctness, latency, complexity, recovery behavior, and fit with the Cortex/agent subject model.
Recommend a direction and name residual risks without starting implementation.
P000.title: Cortex incremental LLM context assembly research

artifacts: Source inspection only; no implementation changes were made for this ticket.
R000.summary: Researched the current novaic-cortex LLM context assembly implementation and compared several ways to move work off the LLM call path or make assembly incremental. Recommendation: do not jump directly to full synchronous write-through context; use a durable projection model, with a practical first phase of historical folded-prefix projection plus active-path on-demand expansion.

acceptance_criteria: The current implementation is described with exact code pointers.
At least four viable options are compared with pros and cons.
The recommendation distinguishes near-term safe improvement from long-term “perfect” architecture.
No code implementation is performed in this ticket.
assumptions: Agent-root DFS remains the semantic source of truth unless deliberately replaced by an event/projection model.
The user is asking for design research, not immediate implementation.
T000.classification_reason: bounded design research with source inspection and no implementation
T000.problem_definition: The user wants a grounded analysis of whether Cortex LLM context assembly can move from full call-time DFS assembly to a non-call-time or incremental model, based on the current code rather than speculation.
T000.proposed_solution:
- Inspect the active prepare chain in Cortex and Runtime.
- Inspect workspace mutation points: append context, write steps, create/complete scopes, notification merge.
- Build a current-state model of when data changes and when prepared context is consumed.
- Design multiple alternatives: keep on-demand, memoized snapshot, synchronous incremental materialization, async outbox projection, and hybrid active-path cache.
- Compare options by correctness, latency, complexity, recovery, and boundary clarity.
risks: Incremental context can create stale snapshots unless invalidation/generation rules are explicit.
Moving work off the LLM call path can hide failures unless projection state is observable.
T000.title: Research incremental Cortex context assembly options
T000.verification_plan:
- Use source inspection with `rg`, `sed`, and line-numbered snippets.
- Confirm no code files are changed by the research ticket.
- Record the final recommendation in the ledger result and summarize to the user.
